title: Should you take a gap year?
explanation:
Should you take a gap year?

For some students, taking a gap year before university can be a valuable experience. It gives them time to travel, work, volunteer or simply think more carefully about what they want to study. Many say they return more independent and motivated. However, a gap year is not automatically useful. Without a plan, students may end up wasting time or spending too much money. Experts recommend setting clear goals, creating a budget and choosing activities that build useful skills rather than just filling time.

questions.text: A gap year happens [*before university|after retirement|during secondary school only].
questions.feedback: That is the context in the passage.

questions.text: Some students return more [*independent and motivated|uncertain and tired|wealthy].
A gap year is not useful [*without a plan|for volunteers|for travellers].
questions.feedback: The text warns against having no plan.

questions.text: Experts recommend creating [*a budget|a business|a timetable for exams only].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A gap year can help students think more carefully about what they want to ____.  | *study | publish | replace
questions.feedback: Choosing a course is one reason.

questions.text: Some people may end up ____ time if the year is not planned well. | *wasting | earning | saving
Experts say students should set clear ____.  | *goals | symptoms | gate numbers
Useful activities can help build important ____.  | *skills | alarms | receipts
The writer's message is that a gap year can be [*helpful if well planned|always a bad idea|only for rich students].
The article focuses on [*advantages and risks|only travel advice|university entrance exams].
Volunteering is presented as one possible [*activity|problem|expense].
The passage would probably appear in [*education advice|a recipe book|a sports report].
